step_1_context.text: I had my car repaired last week. I didn't fix it myself — the mechanic did it for me.

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: Use the causative to show that someone else does an action for you.
step_2_explanation.formula: Have + object + past participle (formal). Get + object + past participle (informal).
step_2_explanation.common_mistake: Do not confuse 'I had my hair cut' (someone cut it for me) with 'I cut my hair' (I did it myself).

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: She ___ her house painted last summer.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Both 'had' and 'got' work in the causative. 'Had' is more formal.

step_3_interactive_exercises.incorrect_sentence: I need to have my phone repair.
step_3_interactive_exercises.corrected_sentence: I need to have my phone repaired.
step_3_interactive_exercises.error_analysis: The causative requires past participle: repaired, not base form.
